summary: Partial verification of the WordPress connection status sync fix on /dashboard/connections. The initial UI state (Not Connected badge, Connect button visible, Disconnect hidden) is CORRECT for both seeded test users. Modal opens correctly via [data-testid=website-card-wordpress-connect-btn]. However, the full fix (Stepper -> Test & Connect -> verify-connection error wording -> success state -> SiteContext refresh -> auto-poll every 5s -> badge flip to Connected/Disconnect link) CANNOT be verified end-to-end because BOTH seeded test users (test_1779193655 and test_1779265489) currently have NO active site — the modal renders the [data-testid=wp-no-site] empty state with the message 'No site selected yet. Please add your website first in the site switcher above, then come back to connect it.' This means the stepper, wp-test-connect button, verify-connection API call, and the auto-poll effect (guarded by `!wpOpen || !activeSite?.id`) are unreachable with the supplied credentials. Iteration 15 already flagged the same gap (admin sees site=maternityfeed.com on this user but the user dashboard does not surface it as activeSite).

minor.issue: Returns an empty list for test_1779193655@jalwa.com (and test_1779265489@jalwa.com) even though /adminpanel/users shows at least one site (maternityfeed.com) attached to the same user. This blocks ALL site-dependent UX (incl. the full WordPress connect flow) for these test users.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot be exercised with the supplied credentials because activeSite is null → modal renders wp-no-site state and the 3-step stepper / wp-test-connect button never mount. Auto-poll useEffect also early-returns due to `!activeSite?.id` guard.

issues: When user has no site, the WordPress card still shows a green 'Connect' button that opens the modal only to show 'No site selected yet'. Consider gating the Connect button on activeSite (or showing 'Add a site first') to avoid the dead-end click.

verified_pass: [data-testid=website-card-wordpress-status-badge] = 'Not Connected' (gray) for both seeded users — correct.
Clicking the Connect button opens [data-testid=wordpress-connect-modal] without errors.
GET /api/sites is called on page load (3 calls observed — SiteProvider hydration + ConnectionsPage / SiteSwitcher).

Code review: WordPressConnectModal.handleTestAndConnect calls `await refresh()` before showing success and toast 'WordPress connected! Articles will publish automatically.' — implementation matches spec.
Code review: ConnectionsPage useEffect sets a 5s interval (max 6 polls) calling refresh() when modal is open AND activeSite?.id exists — matches spec.
Code review: isWpConnected derives from activeSite.wordpressConnected | wordpress_connected | isConnected | connected | status==='connected' and feeds into setWebsitePlatforms — correct sync logic.
Code review: WordPress Disconnect path inside ConnectionsPage.disconnectWebsite triggers `toast.info('Disconnect coming soon — contact support to remove a site.')` — matches spec.

Code review: error message default in handleTestAndConnect (line 153) reads 'Connection failed. Make sure you pasted the API key in your WordPress plugin and clicked Verify & Connect there first.' — matches spec wording exactly.
unverified_due_to_no_site: wp-test-connect click → /api/sites/{id}/verify-connection → error toast wording (could not trigger network call).
wp-success state + success toast wording (could not trigger).

Auto-poll: 5s interval × 6 polls of GET /api/sites while modal open (guarded by activeSite?.id, so 0 polls observed in 12s window with no-site user — expected with current guard, but spec-level behaviour not verified).
Badge flip from 'Not Connected' → 'Connected' on success.
Disconnect link appearing post-connect + its toast.
action_items: HIGH (testability): Provide a seeded test user that already has at least one site attached AND whose /api/sites returns it (so SiteContext.activeSite is non-null). Without this, the WordPress connect flow can never be black-box tested. The discrepancy between /adminpanel showing maternityfeed.com on test_1779193655 vs GET /api/sites returning empty for the same user is a backend bug that should be RCA'd (likely a user_id / owner mismatch or soft-deleted site filter).

MEDIUM (UX): Consider disabling [data-testid=website-card-wordpress-connect-btn] (or routing to /dashboard/onboarding) when activeSite is null, so users don't land on the wp-no-site dead-end inside the modal.

LOW (a11y): Console warnings 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for DialogContent' on the WordPress modal — add a <DialogDescription> (or aria-describedby) under DialogTitle to silence the Radix warning.
critical_code_review_comments: WordPressConnectModal.handleTestAndConnect — refresh() is wrapped in try/catch and `await`ed BEFORE showing the success toast. Good pattern, ensures the dashboard outside the modal reflects the new state before the user reads the toast.

ConnectionsPage auto-poll effect cleans up the interval correctly on unmount/dep-change (return () => clearInterval(interval)). Good. Note: poll loop only stops on maxPolls — it does NOT stop early when isWpConnected flips true. Consider adding `if (isWpConnected) clearInterval(interval)` to avoid an unnecessary GET /api/sites after the user is already connected.

isWpConnected supports 5 different flag shapes (wordpressConnected / wordpress_connected / isConnected / connected / status==='connected') — defensive but slightly fragile. Pick one canonical backend contract and document it; otherwise a future backend rename will silently regress.

Disconnect path is hard-coded to a toast ('Disconnect coming soon…') because no backend endpoint exists. Acceptable as documented in PRD, but should add a TODO comment pointing to the future DELETE /api/sites/{id}/wordpress endpoint.

success_rate.frontend: Initial-state checks 100% PASS (3/3). Full connect-flow checks 0/5 EXERCISED (blocked by missing activeSite for both seeded users). Code review: 100% match with spec.

test_credentials: test_1779193655@jalwa.com / TestPassword123! — login works, but GET /api/sites returns empty so activeSite=null. Same for test_1779265489@jalwa.com.
context_for_next_testing_agent: To finish verifying the WordPress connect status-sync fix, the next agent needs a test user whose GET /api/sites returns ≥1 site (so SiteContext.activeSite is set). Once that is available, repeat: (1) open WP modal, (2) step1→2→3, (3) wait for wp-api-key to populate, (4) click wp-test-connect, (5) capture /api/sites/{id}/verify-connection response — assert that on HTTP error the wp-verify-error reads 'Connection failed. Make sure you pasted the API key in your WordPress plugin and clicked Verify & Connect there first.', (6) on success assert wp-success renders, success toast 'WordPress connected! Articles will publish automatically.' shows, (7) with modal still open record GETs to /api/sites for 12s — expect ≥2, (8) confirm [data-testid=website-card-wordpress-status-badge] flips to 'Connected' and disconnect-btn appears, (9) click disconnect and assert toast 'Disconnect coming soon — contact support to remove a site.'.
rca_of_the_issue: Per code inspection the fix code is correct (refresh() awaited, polling effect in place, error/success wording matches spec). Runtime verification is blocked because GET /api/sites returns 0 items for both seeded users — most likely a backend ownership filter mismatch (site exists in DB but its owner_id ≠ authenticated user's id) since /adminpanel shows the site on the same user. Main agent should run: `curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' <BASE>/api/sites` for test_1779193655 and compare to the admin record. Until /api/sites returns the user's site, the WP connect flow remains untestable via UI.
